steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence introduces Marcus Eady's poem as a second example of ekphrastic poetry alongside Rebecca Alexander's poem. The blank must connect these two examples logically. Because both poems do the same thing — take a painting as their subject — the relationship between them is similarity, not contrast or causation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — nevertheless,: Signals contrast or concession — as in "despite what was just said." The two poems are parallel examples, not contrasting ones. ✗
  • Choice B — conversely,: Signals that the second point is the opposite of the first. Eady's poem does the same thing Alexander's does, not the opposite. ✗
  • Choice C — likewise,: Means "in the same way" — exactly right for introducing a parallel example of the same practice. ✓
  • Choice D — hence,: Signals that the second point is a result or consequence of the first. Eady's poem is not caused by Alexander's poem; they are independent examples of the same form. ✗
That matches Choice C — likewise,.
